1. Introduction:
This project aims to develop a virus that can disable antivirus software and other security measures, allowing it to bypass detection and infiltrate target systems undetected. The purpose of this virus is not to cause harm or engage in malicious activities but rather to serve as an educational tool for understanding how such malware operates and how security measures can be circumvented.
2. Overview:
The virus will consist of two main components: the payload (malicious code) and the delivery mechanism (a specially crafted file or email attachment). The payload will aim to disable antivirus software, while the delivery mechanism will be designed to bypass security measures like firewalls
